questions.q: In which Indian state are the Dilwara Temples located?
questions.explanation: The Dilwara Temples are located at Mount Abu in the Sirohi district of Rajasthan.

questions.q: The Dilwara Temples are situated at which famous hill station?
questions.explanation: The temples stand at Mount Abu, the only hill station in Rajasthan, in the Aravalli Range.

questions.q: The Dilwara Temples are sacred to followers of which religion?
questions.explanation: The Dilwara Temples are a renowned group of Jain temples dedicated to the Tirthankaras.

questions.q: What material is the Dilwara Temples' famous carving primarily made from?
questions.explanation: The temples are celebrated for their astonishingly intricate carving in white marble.

questions.q: The Dilwara Temples are most famous for which artistic quality?
questions.explanation: The temples are world-famous for their delicate, lace-like marble carving and ornamentation.

questions.q: Mount Abu, where the Dilwara Temples stand, lies within which mountain range?
questions.explanation: Mount Abu is the highest peak of the Aravalli Range in western India.

questions.q: Roughly during which centuries were the Dilwara Temples built?
questions.explanation: The five Dilwara temples were built between the 11th and 13th centuries CE.

questions.q: How many main temples make up the Dilwara group?
questions.explanation: The Dilwara complex comprises five marble temples, each dedicated to a Jain Tirthankara.

questions.q: The figures worshipped in the Dilwara Temples, the Tirthankaras, are central to which faith?
questions.explanation: Tirthankaras are the supreme spiritual teachers of Jainism, to whom the Dilwara shrines are dedicated.

questions.q: What is a common nickname for the Dilwara Temples' delicate stonework?
questions.explanation: The translucent, finely cut marble is often described as 'marble lacework' for its lace-like delicacy.

questions.q: The oldest of the Dilwara Temples, the Vimal Vasahi, is dedicated to which Tirthankara?
questions.explanation: The Vimal Vasahi, the earliest Dilwara temple, is dedicated to Adinatha, the first Jain Tirthankara.

questions.q: In which century was the Vimal Vasahi temple, the oldest at Dilwara, built?
questions.explanation: The Vimal Vasahi was built in 1031 CE, making it the earliest of the Dilwara temples.

questions.q: Who commissioned the Vimal Vasahi, the oldest Dilwara temple?
options: Vimal Shah, a minister of the Solanki rulers
questions.explanation: The Vimal Vasahi was built by Vimal Shah, a minister and general of the Chaulukya (Solanki) kings of Gujarat.

questions.q: The Luna Vasahi (Luniga Vasahi) temple at Dilwara is dedicated to which Tirthankara?
questions.explanation: The Luna Vasahi temple is dedicated to Neminatha, the 22nd Jain Tirthankara.

questions.q: Who funded the Luna Vasahi temple at Dilwara?
options: The merchant-minister brothers Vastupala and Tejpala
questions.explanation: The Luna Vasahi was built in 1230 CE by the wealthy brothers Vastupala and Tejpala, ministers of the Vaghela rulers.

questions.q: A celebrated feature of the Dilwara Temples is the ornate ceiling supported by elaborately carved pillars in which hall?
questions.explanation: The Rang Mandapa, a domed central hall, is famed for its intricately carved ceiling and ring of ornate pillars.

questions.q: Surrounding the main shrine of each Dilwara temple is a corridor lined with numerous small cells, each containing what?
questions.explanation: The temples have a cloistered courtyard of small cells (devakulikas), each housing an idol of a Tirthankara.

questions.q: The Vimal Vasahi temple was built under the patronage of which dynasty of Gujarat?
questions.explanation: Vimal Shah served the Chaulukya (Solanki) rulers of Gujarat, under whose era the temple was constructed.

questions.q: What is notably understated about the Dilwara Temples' exterior compared to their interiors?
options: The plain, simple exterior hides lavishly carved interiors
The exterior is gilded while interiors are bare
The exterior is taller than any temple in India
The exterior is built of glass
questions.explanation: The temples have deliberately plain exteriors, concealing the extraordinarily ornate marble carving within.

questions.q: From roughly how far away was the marble transported to build the Dilwara Temples on Mount Abu?
options: From quarries hauled up the mountain by elephants
It was made of artificial cast stone
questions.explanation: Marble was quarried in the plains and laboriously hauled up Mount Abu, traditionally said to be carried by elephants.

questions.q: Which of these is one of the five Dilwara Temples?
questions.explanation: The Pittalhar temple, named for its brass (pittal) Tirthankara image, is one of the five Dilwara temples.

questions.q: The Pittalhar temple at Dilwara takes its name from a large idol cast in which material?
options: Brass (an alloy of copper and zinc)
questions.explanation: The name Pittalhar comes from 'pittal' (brass), referring to its massive metal image of Adinatha.

questions.q: In which exact year was the Vimal Vasahi temple completed?
questions.explanation: The Vimal Vasahi, the oldest Dilwara temple, is dated to 1031 CE.

questions.q: Vastupala and Tejpala, builders of the Luna Vasahi, served as ministers under which dynasty?
questions.explanation: The brothers Vastupala and Tejpala were ministers of the Vaghela rulers of Gujarat in the 13th century.

questions.q: The Luna Vasahi temple is especially admired for an exquisitely carved central dome featuring what motif?
options: A lotus-like pendant carved hanging from the dome
questions.explanation: The Luna Vasahi dome is renowned for an intricately carved central pendant resembling a lotus suspended from the ceiling.

questions.q: Which Dilwara temple is dedicated to Parshvanatha and is the tallest of the group?
questions.explanation: The Khartar Vasahi, dedicated to Parshvanatha, is the tallest of the five Dilwara temples.

questions.q: A distinctive technique behind Dilwara's lace-like effect was that artisans were reportedly paid based on what?
options: The amount of marble dust scraped away
questions.explanation: Tradition holds that carvers were paid by the weight of marble dust they removed, encouraging ever finer, deeper carving.

questions.q: The Sirohi district, home of the Dilwara Temples, borders which neighbouring state?
questions.explanation: Sirohi district lies in southwestern Rajasthan, adjoining the state of Gujarat near Mount Abu.

questions.q: The Vimal Vasahi temple includes an open pavilion outside the main hall featuring stone elephants, known as the:
questions.explanation: The Hathishala, or elephant cell, is a pavilion in the Vimal Vasahi with a row of finely carved marble elephants.

questions.q: The Dilwara Temples' style of richly ornamented marble temple architecture is most associated with which broad architectural tradition?
questions.explanation: Dilwara exemplifies the Maru-Gurjara (Solanki) style of western Indian temple architecture, noted for its profuse marble carving.
